candidates.name: perchloroethylene
candidates.promotion: One curation query from the gate — needs a manual CTD-PD DirectEvidence check (the CTD batch API is ALTCHA-walled as of 2026-07).
candidates.experiment: Confirm CTD-PD DirectEvidence (marker/mechanism); if absent, run a targeted iPSC dopaminergic-neuron assay of PCE against the TCE positive control.
candidates.rationale: Dry-cleaning/degreasing solvent named alongside TCE (a confirmed positive) as a PD risk; distributes to brain and shares a toxic metabolite with TCE. The flagship pending candidate.

evidence.detail: Named as a PD-risk dry-cleaning/degreasing solvent alongside TCE.
Shares a toxic metabolite with TCE; complex-I inhibition at high dose.

candidates.name: fenpyroximate
candidates.promotion: Clears the gate on verification — a registered prototypical stressor on AOP-3 (complex-I MIE 888), the exact MVP scaffold Aitiome anchors.
candidates.experiment: Confirm the AOP-3 stressor registration is in-scope, then promote — no new assay required.
candidates.rationale: Acaricide and registered complex-I (AOP-3) stressor on the endorsed scaffold.

evidence.detail: Registered prototypical stressor on AOP-3 (complex-I inhibition → nigrostriatal degeneration).

candidates.name: tebufenpyrad
candidates.promotion: Clears on verification — registered AOP-3 (complex-I) stressor on the MVP scaffold.
candidates.experiment: Confirm AOP-3 in-scope registration, then promote.
candidates.rationale: Acaricide complex-I inhibitor registered as an AOP-3 stressor.

candidates.name: trifluralin
candidates.promotion: Close — explicit mitochondrial mechanism; a natural AOP-3 expansion candidate.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-PD; test complex-I inhibition to register it as an AOP-3 stressor.
candidates.rationale: Herbicide; iPSC-toxic driver of dopaminergic death identified as causing mitochondrial dysfunction; PWAS-associated.

evidence.detail: Identified as a driver of dopaminergic toxicity via mitochondrial dysfunction.
Toxic in the iPSC dopaminergic-neuron screen.

candidates.name: diquat dibromide
candidates.promotion: Close — paraquat-analog redox mechanism mapping to the AOP-3 mitochondrial key event; curate CTD-PD.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-PD DirectEvidence; the redox/mito mechanism already maps to the AOP-3 KE.
candidates.rationale: Redox-cycling bipyridyl herbicide (paraquat structural cousin); 2nd-most-toxic in the iPSC screen; PWAS-associated.

candidates.name: propargite
candidates.promotion: Close — strongest cellular evidence; needs CTD-PD DirectEvidence or AOP registration.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-PD DirectEvidence; the iPSC dopaminergic toxicity is already in hand.
candidates.rationale: Acaricide; the most potent hit in the iPSC dopaminergic-neuron screen; PWAS-associated with PD.

evidence.detail: Most toxic in the iPSC dopaminergic screen (cell death >3 SD at 30 µM).

candidates.name: picoxystrobin
candidates.promotion: Clears on verification — registered AOP-587 stressor (complex III), pending in-scope confirmation of the EFSA 2024 PD AOP network.
candidates.experiment: Confirm AOP-587 is in-scope for the PD network, then promote.
candidates.rationale: Strobilurin fungicide; registered prototypical stressor on AOP-587 (complex-III inhibition → nigrostriatal degeneration).

evidence.detail: Registered prototypical stressor on AOP-587 (complex-III → nigrostriatal degeneration; EFSA 2024 PD network).

candidates.name: endosulfan
candidates.promotion: High promotion potential — an established organochlorine neurotoxicant missing from the set; likely CTD-curatable now.
candidates.experiment: Curate CTD-PD DirectEvidence (likely already present in the literature).
candidates.rationale: Established organochlorine neurotoxicant (Stockholm POP); iPSC-toxic and PWAS-associated.

evidence.detail: Toxic in the iPSC dopaminergic-neuron screen.

candidates.name: naled
candidates.promotion: Two independent cellular lines converge (iPSC + zebrafish); curate CTD-PD.
candidates.rationale: Organophosphate insecticide; iPSC-toxic and induced aminergic neuron loss in zebrafish; PWAS-associated.

evidence.detail: Toxic in the iPSC dopaminergic screen (top-4 potency).
Induced aminergic neuron loss in zebrafish.

candidates.name: dicofol
candidates.promotion: Two strong lines (epidemiology + cell); curate CTD-PD.
candidates.rationale: Organochlorine acaricide; iPSC-toxic AND a top-FDR PWAS hit — strong on both epidemiology and cell assays.

evidence.detail: Toxic in the iPSC dopaminergic screen.

candidates.name: simvastatin
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated, epidemiological, or mechanistic PD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: troglitazone
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated PD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: warfarin
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated PD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: fenofibrate
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated PD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: prochloraz
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated PD signal. Must rank last.

candidates.name: propiconazole
candidates.experiment: None. Carried as a permanent negative control.
candidates.rationale: Adversarial mitochondria-active decoy — bioactive but non-neurotoxic; no curated PD signal. Must rank last.

backtest.method: TCE's curated CTD-PD DirectEvidence was withheld; it was scored on its convergent non-curated strands alone, then compared to the best adversarial-decoy score.

evidence.detail: Camp Lejeune cohort OR 1.70 for Parkinson's.
